title: Rankett Direct-Offer Sales Script — 30-45 min discovery-to-close call playbook
html:
Rankett Direct-Offer Sales Script — 30-45 min discovery-to-close call playbook

Source: 2025-12-30 Google Doc. The complete word-for-word sales script for Rankett's "Core Offer" direct-to-business calls — 8 sections, opening through close, with reasoning explained. Written 11 days after the 12 month business plan established the $3,500/mo Core Offer tier.

TL;DR

  • Structure (30-45 min):
    1. Opening & Context (3 min) — build rapport, set frame.
    2. Live Demo (8 min) — show proof of concept.
    3. Show Their World (5 min) — demonstrate their challenge.
    4. Present The Solution (5 min) — show responsibilities + what they do.
    5. Show The Terms (3 min) — price, commitment, flexibility.
    6. Prove The Math (5 min) — ROI calculator.
    7. Risk Removal (3 min) — guarantee.
    8. Close (3 min) — next steps.
  • Section 1 opening line (verbatim): "Hey [Name], thanks for taking the time today. Before we jump into anything, I want to be transparent about what this call is. I'm going to show you something specific about how [dentists/roofers/home services] are getting found in AI search right now, and how you specifically compare to your competitors..."
  • Section 1 calibration question: "Are you currently working with an SEO guy or marketing agency on anything, or is this completely fresh for you?" — branches the script (existing vendor → "we complement" frame; no vendor → "we're your AI visibility expert").
  • Section 2 — the nuclear weapon: live demo of Rankett's tool analyzing their business across 30+ AI search prompts vs 2-3 named competitors. Output: "You're appearing in 12 of them. Competitor A is appearing in 18..."
  • The dollar-translation move: *"Every month, probably 50-100 people in Eugene are asking AI 'Who's the best [service]?' Right now, you're showing up about 40% of the time. Competitor A is showing up 60% of the time. That's where your business is leaking."*
  • The lock-in question: "Does this make sense so far? And can you see how if we could move you from 40% to 80% of these prompts, that'd be meaningful for you?" — permission-based selling, gets them to verbalize value.
  • Reasoning blocks under each section — Yahir explains his own sales logic to himself: "transparent about what this is" = removes fear of being sold; "live data, not slides" = destroys 'is this real?' doubt; "show their specific competitor gap" = emotionally invested (competitor is beating them).

Pattern observed

This is Rankett's sales operationalization — completes the trilogy: strategy (Dec 19) → fulfillment ops (Feb 7) → this sales script (Dec 30). By the end of December 2025, Rankett has the strategy + fulfillment + sales trilogy completed in writing, ready to execute against in 2026.

The script's quality reveals Yahir's internalization of conversion-rate-optimization sales: every line has a purpose, every transition is engineered, every objection is preempted. Compare to the much rawer sales-thinking in the Diddles call 3 days later (where the focus is partner-onboarding mechanics). This Dec 30 script is for direct sales; Diddles is for agency partner sales. Different muscles.

Also: the example geography is "Eugene" — not Austin. Suggests Yahir was either targeting Eugene-area businesses (where Emily lives — coincidence? or test market?) or just using a familiar city for the script template.

Key quote

"Show you know why they're losing = Proves you have a solution (not just a problem diagnosis)."

Open questions

  • Has the script been tested against real prospects? Conversion rate?
  • The "30+ prompts" tool — is this Rankett's audit tool or a separate analysis script?
  • Why Eugene as the example city? Connection to Emily?
